input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Install MAUI (pulls in Android, Windows SDK dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows (WinUI 3) apps require the Windows App SDK, which needs VS Build Tools or full VS:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# From an elevated prompt — install MAUI + UWP/WinUI components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs a basic Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and discover what was installed:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK landed
$sdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $sdkPath)) {
    # Workload-managed path (common with dotnet-managed Android)
    $sdkPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK path: $sdkPath"

# List installed platforms and build tools dynamically
& "$sdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
  Select-String "Installed"

# Accept all licenses
& "$sdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level (e.g., 34) if missing
& "$sdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

### Set environment variables (persistent):

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $sdkPath, "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" |
   Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName, "User")
```

## Step 5 — Android Emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
& "$sdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$sdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"
```

> **Prerequisite:** Enable Hyper-V or WHPX in Windows Features for emulator acceleration:
> ```powershell
> # Elevated prompt
> Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
> ```

## Step 6 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI 3 apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create and Build a Test Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI 3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# List available TFMs from your project file
Select-Xml -Path MauiTestApp.csproj `
  -XPath "//TargetFrameworks" |
  ForEach-Object { $_.Node.InnerText -split ";" }

# Find installed Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
  Select-Object Name

# Find installed Android API levels
Get-ChildItem "$sdkPath\platforms" | Select-Object Name

# Check available .NET MAUI templates and their TFMs
dotnet new maui --list
```

---

**Summary of install order:**
1. .NET SDK (`winget`) → 2. MAUI workload (`dotnet workload install`) → 3. VS / Build Tools (for WinUI) → 4. Android SDK licenses + extras → 5. Emulator + Hyper-V → 6. Developer Mode → 7. Verify with a test build.

Adjust `net9.0` to `net8.0` if you prefer LTS. Run `dotnet --list-sdks` to confirm which version you have.
